description:
You are a Compliance Officer at a regional credit union. An alert was triggered indicating that several loan account records were modified outside of normal business hours. Before quarterly regulatory filing, you must investigate this incident using Oracle Flashback technology to determine what changed, restore the corrupted records, document your findings, and implement safeguards.

Your tasks:

1. READ THE INCIDENT REPORT: Open `/home/ga/Desktop/incident_report.txt` to find the approximate time window and the pre-tampering System Change Number (SCN) recorded just before the alert fired.

2. INVESTIGATE TAMPERING: Using Oracle SQL Developer, connect to the LENDING schema (Credentials: lending_admin / Lending2024, Service: XEPDB1). Use Flashback Versions Query (VERSIONS BETWEEN) and Flashback Query (AS OF SCN) to identify exactly which loan records were modified and what their original values were. Focus on changes to `interest_rate`, `current_balance`, and `status`.

3. DOCUMENT FINDINGS: Create a table called `INVESTIGATION_FINDINGS` in the LENDING schema:
